goal: Bundle the Friday demo scene into an offline-capable package (flattened references, pinned Isaac Sim version, git tag) so Erik can roll back in 10 seconds on the demo laptop.
thoughts: zip of the project folder is lossy — Nucleus-referenced prims fail to resolve offline. export_scene_package with embed-references is the real fix. Pin Isaac Sim 5.0.x in a README that ships with the bundle (cross-version USD loads silently mutate schemas). USDZ survives MDL materials partially — test before betting on it. git tag the demo-day commit, not just a local snapshot. Offline acceptance test: unplug wifi, reopen the package, play the scene.
code:
# 1. Flatten all references into a single offline-capable package
export_scene_package(
    source_stage="<DEMO_STAGE>",
    output_path="<BUNDLE_DIR>/demo_frozen_<DATE>.usdz",
    embed_references=True,
    include_textures=True,
    include_mdl=True
)

# 2. Sanity check the packed bundle — nothing should still point at live Nucleus
scene_summary(stage_path="<BUNDLE_DIR>/demo_frozen_<DATE>.usdz")
nucleus_browse(path="<BUNDLE_DIR>/demo_frozen_<DATE>.usdz", list_broken_references=True)

# 3. Confirm USDZ/MDL survival caveats before trusting it on stage
lookup_knowledge(query="USDZ MDL material packing Isaac Sim 5.0 offline texture resolution")

# Ship-list for the demo laptop:
#  - demo_frozen_<DATE>.usdz
#  - README.txt pinning Isaac Sim == 5.0.x (exact patch)
#  - git tag demo-<DATE> on the repo (NOT just a local snapshot)
#  - one-page runbook: open .usdz, press Play, expected pick-and-place timing
# Acceptance: unplug wifi on the demo laptop, reopen .usdz, play for 30s.
failure_modes: zip of project folder — Nucleus paths fail offline, demo laptop shows grey blocks
No pinned Isaac Sim version — next minor release silently mutates MDL shader nodes
No offline test — discovers the broken reference on the customer's wifi-less projector
USDZ packed without MDL — materials drop to default grey at customer site
